requirements.title: Frame type
requirements.text: The system shall define a CAN frame type carrying ID, flags (Ext, RTR, FD, BRS, ESI, XL), and data payload.

requirements.title: Filter type
requirements.text: The system shall define a Filter type for masked ID comparison (id & mask == filter.id & mask).

requirements.title: Bus interface
requirements.text: The system shall expose a Bus trait with Send, Subscribe, and Close methods that are object-safe and Send+Sync.

requirements.title: Frame validation
requirements.text: The system shall validate CAN frames per RELAY spec §15.1 before any transmission; validation shall be performed in validate_frame() and called by every transport send path.

requirements.title: Error sentinels
requirements.text: The system shall expose Closed, NotConnected, Timeout, and PayloadTooLarge error variants as distinct enum members.

requirements.title: Concurrent safety
requirements.text: The Bus trait implementation shall be safe to call from multiple async tasks concurrently: send() and subscribe() shall not deadlock or corrupt shared state when called in parallel. Acceptance criterion: a stress test with N>=4 parallel senders on the same bus completes with 0 panics and all frames received.

requirements.title: RELAY adapter
requirements.text: The system shall expose adapt(), to_message(), and from_message() for RELAY protocol interoperability; to_message/from_message shall be inverse operations (round-trip identity).

requirements.title: Lifecycle
requirements.text: Close shall be idempotent (multiple calls return Ok); send() and subscribe() after close() shall return Error::Closed.

requirements.title: Standard ID range
requirements.text: validate_frame() shall return Error::InvalidFrame for standard (non-extended) IDs exceeding 0x7FF.

requirements.title: Extended ID range
requirements.text: validate_frame() shall return Error::InvalidFrame for extended IDs exceeding 0x1FFFFFFF.

requirements.title: BRS/FD constraint
requirements.text: validate_frame() shall return Error::InvalidFrame for frames with BRS=true and FD=false.

requirements.title: RTR/FD constraint
requirements.text: validate_frame() shall return Error::InvalidFrame for frames with RTR=true and FD=true.

requirements.title: Data length constraint
requirements.text: validate_frame() shall return Error::InvalidFrame for data payloads exceeding the frame-format maximum: classic CAN >8 bytes, CAN FD >64 bytes, CAN XL >2048 bytes.

requirements.title: XL/FD mutual exclusion
requirements.text: validate_frame() shall return Error::InvalidFrame for frames with both XL=true and FD=true.

requirements.title: Convert CLI command
requirements.text: The CLI binary shall implement a convert subcommand per RELAY spec §11.2: read a can.Frame JSON object from stdin, convert it via to_message(), and write the relay.Message JSON on stdout; exit 0 on success, 1 on invalid input, 2 on invalid protocol.

requirements.title: Golden vector conformance
requirements.text: The RELAY adapter shall produce relay.Message JSON that exactly matches the canonical golden vectors in testdata/relay-vectors/ for all CAN frame types (classic, FD, XL), and shall reject all error vectors with Error::InvalidFrame.

requirements.title: Candump record
requirements.text: record() shall subscribe to a Bus and write each received frame as a candump-format line to the provided writer until the context is cancelled or the bus closes.

requirements.title: Candump replay
requirements.text: replay() shall parse a candump log from a reader and re-send each frame to the Bus, preserving inter-frame timing scaled by speedFactor; malformed lines are skipped silently.

requirements.title: Candump line format
requirements.text: parse_line() shall parse both classic CAN (ID#DATA) and CAN FD (ID##FLAGSDATA) candump lines; format_line() shall produce the matching textual form.

requirements.title: OBD-II client
requirements.text: Client::new() shall create an OBD-II client over an IsoTpConn; OBD-II uses fixed CAN IDs 0x7DF (request) / 0x7E8 (response).

requirements.title: OBD-II PID read
requirements.text: read_pid() shall send a Mode 01 request and return the decoded Value; it shall return an error for negative responses or malformed replies.

requirements.title: OBD-II VIN read
requirements.text: read_vin() shall send a Mode 09 PID 0x02 request and return the VIN string; it shall return an error for negative responses.

requirements.title: UDS client
requirements.text: Client::new() shall create a UDS client over an IsoTpConn.

requirements.title: UDS DiagnosticSessionControl
requirements.text: diagnostic_session_control() shall send SID 0x10 and verify the positive response.

requirements.title: UDS ECUReset
requirements.text: ecu_reset() shall send SID 0x11 and verify the positive response.

requirements.title: UDS TesterPresent
requirements.text: tester_present() shall send SID 0x3E 0x00 and verify the positive response.

requirements.title: UDS ReadDataByIdentifier
requirements.text: read_data_by_identifier() shall send SID 0x22 with the DID and return the payload.

requirements.title: UDS WriteDataByIdentifier
requirements.text: write_data_by_identifier() shall send SID 0x2E with the DID and data.

requirements.title: UDS SecurityAccess
requirements.text: security_access() shall send SID 0x27 sub-functions for seed request and key send.

requirements.title: UDS negative response
requirements.text: request() shall surface NRC 0x78 (response pending) by retrying, and return NegativeResponseError for all other negative responses.

requirements.title: UDS transport
requirements.text: All UDS requests and responses shall be transported over IsoTpConn (ISO 15765-2).

requirements.title: Virtual bus creation
requirements.text: VirtualBus::new() shall succeed without any OS or hardware dependencies (pure in-process).

requirements.title: Frame broadcast
requirements.text: Frames sent on the VirtualBus shall be delivered to every subscriber whose filters match.

requirements.title: Multiple subscribers
requirements.text: VirtualBus shall support at least 64 concurrent subscribers with independent receive channels.

requirements.title: Filter semantics
requirements.text: VirtualBus shall deliver a frame to a subscriber if and only if: the subscriber has no filters, or (frame.id & filter.mask) == (filter.id & filter.mask) for at least one filter.

requirements.title: Back-pressure
requirements.text: VirtualBus shall apply the subscriber's declared back-pressure policy (DropNewest, DropOldest, or Block) when its channel is full.

requirements.title: Single frame
requirements.text: IsoTpConn.send() shall transmit payloads of 1–7 bytes in a single CAN frame with PCI byte N_PCI=0x0N.

requirements.title: Multi-frame send
requirements.text: IsoTpConn.send() shall segment payloads >7 bytes into a first frame (PCI=0x10) followed by consecutive frames (PCI=0x21..0x2F cycling).

requirements.title: Flow control
requirements.text: IsoTpConn.send() shall pause after the first frame and resume transmission only after receiving a flow-control frame (PCI=0x30); block size and ST_min from the FC frame shall be honoured.

requirements.title: Multi-frame receive
requirements.text: IsoTpConn.recv() shall reassemble consecutive frames into the original payload, sending a flow-control frame after the first frame.

requirements.title: Timeout
requirements.text: IsoTpConn.recv() and the internal flow-control wait shall return Error::Timeout when the caller-supplied context deadline expires; the timeout value shall default to 250 ms when the config timeout is zero.

requirements.title: 29-bit ID decode
requirements.text: decode_id() shall extract priority (bits 28–26), data page (bit 24), PDU Format (bits 23–16), PDU Specific (bits 15–8), and source address (bits 7–0) from a 29-bit extended CAN ID.

requirements.title: PGN peer-to-peer
requirements.text: When PDU Format (PF) < 240, decode_id() shall treat PS as the destination address and exclude it from the PGN value.

requirements.title: PGN broadcast
requirements.text: When PDU Format (PF) >= 240, decode_id() shall treat PS as the group extension and include it in the PGN value.

requirements.title: ID encode
requirements.text: encode_id() shall produce a 29-bit extended CAN ID such that decode_id(encode_id(p, pgn, src, dst)) returns (p, pgn, src) with dst recoverable from the PS field.

requirements.title: J1939 send/receive
requirements.text: J1939Bus.send() shall encode J1939 frame fields into a 29-bit extended CAN ID and transmit via the underlying Bus. J1939Bus.subscribe() shall decode received frames and present them as J1939Frame values.

requirements.title: PGN filter
requirements.text: J1939Receiver shall silently discard received frames whose PGN does not match any entry in the subscriber's PGN filter list; an empty list shall pass all frames.

requirements.title: E2E header
requirements.text: Protector.protect() shall prepend a 10-byte E2E header containing DataID (2 bytes), SourceID (2 bytes), SequenceCounter (4 bytes), and CRC-16 (2 bytes) to every payload.

requirements.title: CRC-16 protection
requirements.text: The E2E header CRC field shall be CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE (polynomial 0x1021, initial value 0xFFFF) computed over the first 8 header bytes (CRC slot treated as zero) concatenated with the payload.

requirements.title: Sequence counter
requirements.text: The E2E header SequenceCounter shall increment by 1 on each call to protect(); the counter shall wrap at 2^32.

requirements.title: CRC validation
requirements.text: Receiver.unwrap() shall return E2EError{kind: CrcMismatch} when the CRC in the header does not match the CRC computed from the received data.

requirements.title: Sequence gap detection
requirements.text: Receiver.unwrap() shall return E2EError{kind: SequenceGap} when the received sequence counter is not exactly last_counter+1 (modulo 2^32) for non-first frames.

requirements.title: Header too short
requirements.text: Receiver.unwrap() shall return E2EError{kind: HeaderTooShort} when the input data is fewer than 10 bytes.

requirements.title: DBC parse
requirements.text: parse() shall parse BO_ message blocks (id, name, DLC, sender) and SG_ signal definitions (name, start_bit, length, byte_order, value_type, factor, offset, min, max, unit) from DBC file text.

requirements.title: Signal decode
requirements.text: DbcDatabase.decode() shall compute physical_value = raw * factor + offset for each signal in a message, extracting the raw value from the frame data using start_bit, length, and byte_order (Intel or Motorola).

requirements.title: Frame ID bounds — injection prevention
requirements.text: All external CAN frame IDs received from untrusted sources shall be validated by validate_frame() before processing; frames with out-of-range IDs shall be rejected with Error::InvalidFrame and shall not be forwarded to any subscriber.

requirements.title: E2E CRC tamper detection
requirements.text: Any modification of a single bit in a protected payload or header shall be detected by Receiver.unwrap() with probability >= 1 - 2^-16 per the CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE Hamming distance properties.

requirements.title: Replay detection
requirements.text: Receiver.unwrap() shall reject frames whose sequence counter does not advance monotonically, preventing replay of previously captured protected frames.

requirements.title: Resource exhaustion prevention
requirements.text: IsoTpConn.recv() shall bound its wait time by the configured timeout (default 250 ms); it shall not block indefinitely regardless of the frame arrival pattern.

requirements.title: Input validation on DBC parse
requirements.text: parse() shall return Error::Other for malformed BO_ or SG_ lines; it shall not panic on any input string.

requirements.title: Cryptographic message authentication interface
requirements.text: The safety module SHALL expose a MessageAuthenticator trait that callers can implement using HMAC-SHA256 or AES-CMAC. The E2E CRC-16 layer MUST be documented as a safety control only (random error detection); it SHALL NOT be represented as a security control against adversarial forgery. Applications operating at IEC 62443 SL-2 or ISO/SAE 21434 CAL-3 SHALL use a keyed MAC in addition to CRC-16.
requirements.rationale: CRC-16 has no keying material; an adversary who observes frames can forge a valid CRC (T-CAN-02, T-CAN-05). A trait-based interface allows callers to plug in HMAC without adding a crypto dependency to rust-CAN itself.

requirements.title: Per-subscriber frame rate limiting
requirements.text: When SubscriberOptions.rate_limit_per_sec is non-zero, the bus SHALL drop frames delivered to that subscriber that exceed the configured rate within any 1-second window. Dropped frames SHALL be counted in the bus drop_count metric. This provides defence-in-depth against frame-flooding DoS attacks (T-CAN-07).

requirements.title: Bus-off state detection and exposure
requirements.text: The bus implementation SHALL detect CAN controller bus-off conditions and return Error::BusOff to the caller. The application SHALL be notified so it can implement a recovery policy (e.g., bus reset, watchdog trigger). Bus-off recovery itself is out of scope for the library.

requirements.title: Streaming JSON send (crossbar spoke)
requirements.text: The 'send' CLI command SHALL accept '--format json' to read newline-delimited relay.Message JSON objects from stdin and send each frame via from_message() to the bus. When '--format json' is used the '--id' and '--data' flags SHALL be optional. This makes rust-CAN a valid RELAY v1.8 crossbar destination.
